skill.name: intent-analyzer
skill.description: Analyzes ambiguous user requests, identifies likely goals and constraints, and recommends focused clarification questions before work begins.
skill.summary: Clarifies intent behind ambiguous requests so assistants can choose better responses.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and network patterns, but review found ordinary instructional prose and Graphviz labels rather than executable behavior. No prompt injection attempt, data exfiltration path, command execution, filesystem access, or real network access was found in the reviewed files. The skill is low risk because the flagged patterns are false positives in a community-authored prompt skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords in Prose
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography matches occur in descriptive text and Graphviz labels, such as request categories and words related to design. No evidence found of cryptographic code, hashing calls, encryption functions, or unsafe algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are prose or diagram labels, not runnable code. The surrounding context discusses intent analysis categories and clarification strategy.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms in Intent Guidance
low_findings.description: The static reconnaissance matches are normal language about mapping intent, constraints, expertise, and clarification. No evidence found of system probing, host enumeration, port scanning, or network discovery instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is instructional guidance for understanding user intent. It does not invoke tools or describe operational reconnaissance against systems or networks.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Library Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported network location is a reminder to clarify only when needed. No evidence found of Python HTTP libraries, URLs, fetch behavior, sockets, or outbound requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact cited line contains general guidance about efficient clarification. It has no network API usage or network-related instruction.

content.value_statement: Ambiguous requests often lead to wasted work, wrong assumptions, and repeated revisions. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code infer likely intent, surface constraints, and ask focused questions.
actual_capabilities: Classifies requests as creative, analytical, technical, learning, decision-making, or problem-solving tasks.
Identifies explicit and implicit constraints that can affect the response.
Estimates when a request is clear enough to proceed or needs clarification.
Suggests one to three focused clarification questions for ambiguous requests.
Reconstructs a clearer interpretation of the user's goal before responding.
limitations: Does not execute code, inspect systems, or gather external data.
Cannot know hidden user intent with certainty when context is missing.
May add unnecessary analysis if applied to simple requests.
Depends on the assistant following the guidance consistently.

use_cases.title: Clarify Product Requests
use_cases.description: Turn vague feature requests into clearer goals, constraints, and next questions before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Improve Coding Assistance
use_cases.description: Distinguish whether a user wants a quick fix, a learning explanation, or production-ready implementation guidance.

use_cases.title: Guide Writing Tasks
use_cases.description: Identify audience, tone, purpose, and output expectations before drafting or rewriting content.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify a Simple Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use for a short ambiguous message.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use intent-analyzer on this request: [paste request]. Tell me the most likely intent and one clarification question if needed.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Between Interpretations
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this request with intent-analyzer: [paste request]. List the top likely interpretations, confidence level, and the best next response.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Constraints
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare for a project, writing, or coding task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply intent-analyzer to this project request: [paste request]. Identify explicit constraints, implied constraints, missing context, and focused questions.

prompt_templates.title: Reconstruct the Goal
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use for high-stakes or multi-step work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use intent-analyzer deeply on this conversation: [paste context]. Reconstruct the user's true goal, assumptions, risk of misunderstanding, and recommended response strategy.

output_examples.output: Likely intent is unclear because the user may want planning, troubleshooting, review, or direct execution. Best next step: ask what the project is and whether they want guidance, feedback, or completed work.
Likely intent is writing refinement. Missing context includes audience, tone, and purpose. Ask whether the user wants it more concise, more formal, more persuasive, or easier to read.
Likely intent is decision support. The response should ask about project requirements, maintenance expectations, compatibility, and alternatives before recommending a choice.

best_practices: Use the skill when ambiguity could change the final answer or implementation approach.
Ask only one to three high-value questions when clarification is needed.
State important assumptions briefly so the user can correct them early.
anti_patterns: Do not apply deep intent analysis to obvious, low-stakes questions.
Do not ask many broad questions when one targeted question would unblock progress.
Do not override the user's explicit correction after they clarify their goal.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an assistant interpret ambiguous requests and decide whether to proceed, state assumptions, or ask clarification questions.

faq.question: Does it make decisions for the user?
faq.answer: No. It helps frame the decision, identify missing context, and choose a response strategy.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it for unclear, complex, high-stakes, or open-ended requests where misunderstanding would waste effort.

faq.question: Can it work with coding tasks?
faq.answer: Yes. It can distinguish debugging, learning, production implementation, and quick scripting intents.

faq.question: Does it access the network or local files?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill content provides prompt guidance only and does not include executable access behavior.

faq.question: What tools support it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
